thread.title: Rotarians hold annual conference

author: JG-TC
title: Rotarians hold annual conference
text:
Rotary District 6490 recently held its annual conference at the Holiday Inn in Champaign.
Rotarians from east central Illinois learned about the variety of Rotary programs that are currently in place and potential opportunities to support.
Janet Grove, immediate past president of the Mattoon Rotary Club, served as emcee for the Friday luncheon and led a breakout session on estate planning and foundation giving.
Bill Lair, from Charleston Rotary Club, led a breakout session on Rotary Youth Exchange.
District Governor Mike Martin, from Mattoon Rotary, presided over the conference.
Immediate Past District Governor Janet Ellis-Nelson presented Tina Stovall, of Mattoon Rotary, with a Make It Happen Award.
Stovall co-chairs the Rotary District 6490 Disaster Response Team. Under her leadership, Rotary created a team of representatives from clubs in east central Illinois, worked to create a disaster response plan and enter skills and inventory that could be needed in an emergency into a database. Stovall met with emergency management personnel, took several trainings conducted by Illinois and Federal Emergency Management Association and led all team meetings.
People are also reading…
Ellis-Nelson presented Kim Denton, from Mattoon Rotary, with the Rotarian of the Year Award for 2023-2024.
Denton was recognized for her efforts in making the Rotary Youth Leadership Awards (RYLA) Camp meaningful and successful for the high school students that attended the camp. As a 4-H youth development educator for the University of Illinois Extension, she led sessions for the National Curriculum and Training Institute on the Real Colors Personality Inventory, which revealed students’ leadership styles. Denton also led a session on business and professional etiquette and facilitated a presentation by Rotary Youth Exchange students. In addition, she acquired all the materials and taught students how to make dog and cat toys, which were donated to area shelters.
Rich Churchman, from Louisiana, served as Rotary International Stephanie Urchick’s president representative. Vice chair of The Rotary Foundation, Larry Lunsford, from Kansas City, Missouri, gave a trustee’s report.
